title: Java Basics & Hello World
description: Learn Java syntax, data types, variables, and write your first Java program.
objectives: Write and run a Java program
exercises: Write a program that prints 'Hello Java'
Create variables of different data types

title: Control Flow Statements
description: Master if-else statements, switch cases, and loops in Java.
exercises: Write a program to check if a number is even or odd

title: Arrays & Strings
description: Work with arrays, strings, and common string operations.
exercises: Find maximum element in an array

title: Methods & Functions
description: Learn to create and use methods with parameters and return values.
objectives: Learn about method parameters and return types
exercises: Create a method to calculate factorial
Implement a method to check palindrome
Create overloaded methods for different data types

title: Object-Oriented Programming Basics
description: Understand classes, objects, constructors, and the 'this' keyword.
objectives: Understand the concept of classes and objects
exercises: Create a Student class with attributes and methods
Create a Rectangle class with area calculation

title: Encapsulation & Access Modifiers
description: Learn about access modifiers and implement encapsulation in Java.
objectives: Understand public, private, protected, and default modifiers
exercises: Create an Employee class with private fields
Implement a BankAccount class with validation
Build a Temperature class with conversion methods

title: Inheritance
description: Understand inheritance, super keyword, and method overriding.
exercises: Create a Vehicle class with Car and Bike subclasses
Build a Shape class with Circle and Rectangle subclasses

title: Polymorphism
description: Learn about compile-time and runtime polymorphism in Java.
exercises: Implement Payment interface with multiple implementations
Build Calculator class with overloaded methods

title: Interfaces
description: Learn about interfaces, multiple inheritance, and default methods.
exercises: Build Payment system with multiple payment methods

title: Exception Handling
description: Learn to handle exceptions using try-catch blocks and custom exceptions.
exercises: Implement file reading with exception handling

title: Collections Framework - List
description: Learn about List interface and its implementations: ArrayList and LinkedList.
exercises: Create a student list management system

title: Collections Framework - Set
description: Learn about Set interface and its implementations: HashSet, TreeSet, and LinkedHashSet.
objectives: Understand Set interface and its properties
Work with HashSet, TreeSet, and LinkedHashSet
Perform set operations like union and intersection
exercises: Remove duplicates from a list using Set
Find common elements between two sets

title: Collections Framework - Map
description: Learn about Map interface and its implementations: HashMap, TreeMap, and LinkedHashMap.
objectives: Understand Map interface and key-value pairs
Work with HashMap, TreeMap, and LinkedHashMap
exercises: Build a student grade management system

title: Generics
description: Learn about generic classes, methods, and type parameters in Java.

title: Input/Output Streams
description: Learn about Java I/O streams for reading and writing data.
objectives: Understand byte streams and character streams
exercises: Build a student data file manager

title: Multithreading Basics
description: Learn about threads, synchronization, and concurrent programming in Java.
exercises: Create a counter with multiple threads

title: Advanced Multithreading
description: Learn about thread pools, executors, and advanced concurrency utilities.
exercises: Create a thread pool for image processing
Build a web scraper with multiple threads

title: Lambda Expressions
description: Learn about functional programming with lambda expressions in Java.

title: Stream API
description: Learn about Java Stream API for functional-style operations on collections.
exercises: Filter and transform a list of products

title: Optional Class
description: Learn to use Optional class to handle null values gracefully in Java.
objectives: Apply Optional methods for safe operations

title: Date and Time API
description: Learn about Java 8+ Date and Time API for modern date/time manipulation.
objectives: Work with LocalDate, LocalTime, and LocalDateTime
exercises: Create a booking system with date validation

title: Java I/O - NIO
description: Learn about Java NIO (New I/O) for non-blocking I/O operations.
objectives: Work with Path and Files classes
exercises: Create a file copy utility using NIO

title: Database Connectivity - JDBC
description: Learn to connect Java applications with databases using JDBC.

title: Unit Testing with JUnit
description: Learn to write and run unit tests using JUnit framework.
exercises: Implement TDD for a bank account system

title: Design Patterns - Creational
description: Learn creational design patterns: Singleton, Factory, Builder, and more.

title: Design Patterns - Structural
description: Learn structural design patterns: Adapter, Decorator, Facade, and more.

title: Design Patterns - Behavioral
description: Learn behavioral design patterns: Observer, Strategy, Command, and more.
exercises: Build a remote control command system

title: Networking in Java
description: Learn network programming with sockets, HTTP clients, and server communication.
objectives: Work with URL and URI classes

title: Java Reflection
description: Learn about Java Reflection API for runtime class inspection and dynamic operations.
objectives: Invoke methods and access fields reflectively

title: Final Project: Build a Complete Application
description: Apply all Java concepts learned to build a complete console-based application.
objectives: Design and implement a complete system
Apply OOP principles and design patterns
Handle file I/O, collections, and exceptions
